items.caption: A slide from a presentation with a blue background and white text. it contains information about electrocardiography (ecg) leads. the title at the top reads "3 lead ecg: bipolar leads of ecg." below the title, there are three bullet points explaining the concept of bipolar leads in ecg. the first point states that an imaginary line connecting any two electrodes is called the "ecg lead." the second point explains that a lead is bipolar when both positive and negative electrodes contribute to the deflection in ecg. the third point describes that the positive and negative electrodes are placed at an equal distance away from the heart and that the resulting ecg deflection is the sum of the electrical forces going in opposite directions. in the bottom right corner, there is a graphic of a heart with a red line through it, which is commonly associated with medical imagery. the overall style of the image is educational and informative, likely used in a medical or healthcare context.
